questions.stem: What does the man want to do?
questions.context:
M: I'd like to exchange this jacket for a larger size.
W: Sure, do you have the receipt?
M: Yes, here it is. I bought it last weekend.
W: Let me check if we have your size in stock.
questions.explanation: The man wants to exchange the jacket for a larger size, as he directly states this request.

options.text: Return a jacket for a refund.
Exchange a jacket for a different size.

questions.stem: What does the woman ask the man to do?
questions.context:
W: Could you pick up the catering order on your way back from the meeting?
M: Sure, which restaurant is it from?
W: Bella's Kitchen on Pine Street. The order is under our company name.
M: Got it. I should be there around noon.
questions.explanation: The woman asks the man to pick up a catering order from a restaurant on his way back.

questions.stem: Where does the man most likely work?
questions.context:
W: I'm having trouble with my laptop screen. It keeps flickering.
M: Let me take a look. When did this start happening?
W: Just this morning. It was fine yesterday.
M: It might be a loose connection. I'll run a quick diagnostic test.
questions.explanation: The man's response—offering to run a diagnostic test on the laptop—indicates he works in IT support or technical support.

questions.stem: What problem does the man mention?
questions.context:
M: I scheduled the conference room for our team meeting, but it looks like someone else already booked it.
W: That happens a lot with the new booking system. Did you try the room on the fifth floor?
M: Not yet. I'll check if it's available.
W: If not, we could always use the lounge area. It should be free.
questions.explanation: The man mentions that the conference room he booked was already reserved by someone else, creating a scheduling conflict.

questions.stem: What will the woman probably do next?
questions.context:
M: The sales figures for last quarter look great. Should we include them in the investor presentation?
W: Absolutely. I'll add a comparison chart showing the year-over-year growth.
M: Good idea. Can you have it ready by three o'clock?
W: I'll start on it right away.
questions.explanation: The woman says she'll start right away on creating a comparison chart for the investor presentation.

options.text: Create a chart for the presentation.
Send the sales report to the manager.

questions.stem: Why is the woman concerned?
questions.context:
W: Have you seen the weather forecast for Saturday? They're predicting heavy rain.
M: That could be a problem for the outdoor charity event.
W: Exactly. We don't have a backup plan for an indoor venue.
M: Let me call the community center to see if they have space available.
questions.explanation: The woman is concerned because there is no indoor backup venue if it rains on the day of the outdoor charity event.

options.text: A delivery was damaged by rain.
An outdoor event has no rain contingency.
The charity raised less money than expected.
A speaker cancelled at the last minute.

questions.stem: What does the man offer to do?
questions.context:
W: I'm not sure how to fill out this expense reimbursement form. It's different from the old one.
M: I just completed mine yesterday. I can walk you through it.
W: That would be great. I especially need help with the receipt attachment section.
M: No problem. Pull up the form and I'll guide you step by step.
questions.explanation: The man offers to help the woman fill out the expense reimbursement form since he just went through the same process.

options.text: Submit the form on her behalf.
Print a copy of the instructions.

questions.stem: What does the woman mean when she says, 'That's cutting it close'?
questions.context:
M: The supplier confirmed they can deliver the materials by Thursday.
W: That's cutting it close. The installation is scheduled for Friday morning.
M: I know, but they assured me delivery by noon at the latest.
W: Let's have a backup supplier ready just in case.
questions.explanation: The woman means that Thursday delivery leaves very little time before the Friday installation, making the timeline risky.

options.text: The delivery is ahead of schedule.
The timeline allows very little margin.
The price is close to their budget.

questions.stem: What does the man suggest?
questions.context:
W: We need to find a way to reduce our shipping costs. They've gone up twenty percent this year.
M: What about negotiating volume discounts with our current carrier?
W: I tried that already, but they weren't willing to lower the rates.
M: Then maybe we should get quotes from other shipping companies.
questions.explanation: After learning the current carrier won't negotiate, the man suggests getting quotes from alternative shipping companies.

questions.stem: What are the speakers discussing?
questions.context:
M: I got an email saying our office internet will be upgraded this weekend.
W: That's good news. The current connection has been really slow lately.
M: They said there might be a brief outage Saturday morning during the switch.
W: That shouldn't be a problem since nobody works on Saturdays.
questions.explanation: The speakers are discussing a planned internet upgrade for their office and a potential brief outage.

questions.stem: What does the woman imply about the deadline?
questions.context:
M: The client wants the revised design by next Wednesday.
W: That's going to be tough. We haven't even received their feedback on the first draft yet.
M: I'll follow up with them today and try to get their comments by tomorrow.
W: Even then, three days might not be enough if they want major changes.
questions.explanation: The woman implies that the Wednesday deadline may not be realistic, especially if the client requests significant revisions.

options.text: It has already been extended once.
It may be difficult to meet.
It was set by the design team.
It is the same as the original deadline.

questions.stem: What does the man say about the new employee?
questions.context:
W: How is the new marketing coordinator doing?
M: She's picking things up really quickly. She already redesigned the product catalog.
W: That's impressive for the first week. Have you assigned her to any client accounts yet?
M: Not yet, but I plan to next week after her orientation is complete.
questions.explanation: The man says the new employee is learning quickly and has already completed a significant task in her first week.

options.text: She was transferred from another branch.
She has already met all the clients.

questions.stem: What will the speakers most likely do next?
questions.context:
W: The trade show starts in two weeks, and we still don't have our booth design finalized.
M: I know. The graphics team has been swamped with other projects.
W: Should we hire an outside design firm to help?
M: That might be the best option at this point. I'll get some proposals by tomorrow.
questions.explanation: The man agrees to get proposals from outside design firms by tomorrow to help finalize the trade show booth.

options.text: Cancel their booth at the trade show.
Contact outside firms for design help.
Ask the graphics team to work overtime.

questions.stem: What does the woman recommend?
questions.context:
M: I'm trying to decide which project management tool to use for the new team.
W: I'd suggest trying Trello. It's intuitive and works well for smaller teams.
M: Is that what your department uses?
W: We switched to it last year, and it's really improved our workflow.
questions.explanation: The woman recommends Trello because her department has had a positive experience with it.

options.text: Using a specific project management tool.

questions.stem: What is the man worried about?
questions.context:
M: I just found out our main competitor dropped their prices by fifteen percent.
W: That's aggressive. Do you think it'll affect our sales?
M: It could, especially with the holiday season coming up. Customers will be comparing prices.
W: Maybe we should focus on emphasizing our product quality and customer service instead of matching their prices.
questions.explanation: The man is worried that the competitor's significant price reduction could negatively impact their sales during the holiday season.

options.text: The impact of a competitor's price cut.
An employee shortage during the holidays.

questions.stem: What does the woman say about the training program?
questions.context:
M: Did you attend the leadership training last Tuesday?
W: Yes, it was very useful. The instructor covered conflict resolution and team motivation techniques.
M: I missed it because of a client meeting. Will it be offered again?
W: They're planning another session next month. I'll forward you the registration link.
questions.explanation: The woman found the training program very useful and mentions that another session will be offered next month.

options.text: It was cancelled due to low attendance.
It was informative and will be repeated.

questions.stem: What does the man plan to do about the feedback?
questions.context:
W: Several customers have complained about the new packaging. They say the boxes are too difficult to open.
M: That's concerning. We invested a lot in the redesign. I'll bring it up at the product review meeting tomorrow.
W: We should also get feedback from the design team on possible modifications.
M: Agreed. I'll schedule a meeting with them for Thursday.
questions.explanation: The man plans to raise the customer complaints at the product review meeting and also schedule a separate meeting with the design team.

options.text: Ignore the complaints and continue production.
Discuss the issue at upcoming meetings.
Return to the original packaging immediately.

questions.stem: Why is the man calling?
questions.context:
M: Hello, I'm calling to check on the status of my order. I placed it online five days ago.
W: I'd be happy to look into that. Could you give me your order number?
M: It's four-seven-eight-three-two-one.
W: Let me pull that up. It looks like your order shipped yesterday and should arrive by Friday.
questions.explanation: The man is calling to check the delivery status of an order he placed five days ago.

questions.stem: What does the woman imply about the report?
questions.context:
M: Have you had a chance to review the quarterly financial report?
W: I started looking at it, but some of the figures in the revenue section don't match our internal records.
M: That's odd. Which numbers are off?
W: I'll send you a marked-up copy this afternoon so you can see the discrepancies.
questions.explanation: The woman implies that the report contains inaccuracies in the revenue section that need to be corrected.

questions.stem: What does the man ask about?
questions.context:
M: I heard the company is offering a tuition reimbursement program. Do you know the details?
W: Yes, they'll cover up to five thousand dollars per year for job-related courses.
M: That's great. Is there a minimum grade requirement?
W: You need to earn at least a B to qualify for reimbursement.
questions.explanation: The man is asking about the details of the company's tuition reimbursement program.

questions.stem: What does the man suggest the woman do?
questions.context:
W: I've been trying to reach the vendor all week, but they never return my calls.
M: Have you tried emailing them? Sometimes they respond faster that way.
W: I did send an email two days ago, but no reply yet.
M: At that point, I'd recommend contacting their regional manager directly. I have his number if you need it.
questions.explanation: The man suggests the woman escalate the issue by contacting the vendor's regional manager directly.

options.text: Visit the vendor's office in person.
Wait another week for a response.

questions.stem: What are the speakers mainly discussing?
questions.context:
M: Have you decided where to hold the year-end awards ceremony?
W: We're looking at two options—the Grand Hotel ballroom or the rooftop venue at the Park Tower.
M: The rooftop sounds nice, but it might be too cold in December.
W: That's a good point. Let me check if the Grand Hotel has any dates open.
questions.explanation: The speakers are discussing the venue selection for the year-end awards ceremony.

options.text: A venue for an awards event.

questions.stem: What does the woman say she needs to do?
questions.context:
M: Are you ready for the product demonstration this afternoon?
W: Almost, but I still need to test the software on the presentation laptop to make sure it runs smoothly.
M: The IT team set it up this morning. It should be in the demo room.
W: I'll go check it now. Better to catch any issues before the client arrives.
questions.explanation: The woman needs to test the software on the presentation laptop before the product demonstration.

options.text: Test software on the demo equipment.

questions.stem: What does the man say about the parking situation?
questions.context:
W: I was fifteen minutes late this morning because I couldn't find a parking space.
M: I know, ever since they closed the north lot for construction, parking has been terrible.
W: Do you know when it will reopen?
M: The facilities department said it should be back open by the end of next month.
questions.explanation: The man explains that parking has been difficult because the north lot is closed for construction, but it should reopen by month's end.

options.text: New parking meters have been installed.
A parking lot is temporarily closed.
The parking garage rates have increased.

questions.stem: What does the woman ask the man to check?
questions.context:
W: I sent out the press release about the acquisition, but I want to make sure the financial details are accurate.
M: Which details are you concerned about?
W: The acquisition price and the expected completion date. Could you verify those with the CFO's office?
M: Sure, I'll call them right now and get back to you.
questions.explanation: The woman asks the man to verify the acquisition price and expected completion date with the CFO's office.

options.text: Financial details in a press release.

questions.stem: What will happen next Tuesday?
questions.context:
M: Don't forget, the office will be inspected for fire safety compliance next Tuesday.
W: Thanks for the reminder. Is there anything we need to do to prepare?
M: Just make sure all hallways and exits are clear of boxes and equipment.
W: I'll send an email to the whole floor about it today.
questions.explanation: A fire safety compliance inspection is scheduled for next Tuesday at their office.

questions.stem: Why does the man say, 'That changes things'?
questions.context:
W: I just heard from the client that they want to add three more product features to the project scope.
M: That changes things. We'll need more time and probably more resources.
W: I know. Should we request a deadline extension?
M: Yes, and we should also adjust our cost estimate before the next meeting.
questions.explanation: The man says 'That changes things' because the added features will require more time, resources, and a revised cost estimate, significantly altering the project plan.

options.text: He is pleased with the additions.
The project scope expansion affects planning.
He wants to remove some features.

questions.stem: Where are the speakers most likely going?
questions.context:
M: The cab should be here in five minutes. Do you have the presentation materials?
W: Yes, I've got everything. The client's office is about a twenty-minute ride from here.
M: Perfect. I also brought extra copies of the proposal in case they need them.
W: Good thinking. Let's head downstairs to the lobby.
questions.explanation: The speakers are about to take a cab to a client's office for a presentation, as they mention presentation materials and the client's location.

questions.stem: What does the woman mean when she says, 'I'm two minds about it'?
questions.context:
M: Have you decided whether to accept the transfer to the Tokyo office?
W: I'm of two minds about it. It's a great career opportunity, but my children are settled in school here.
M: That's a tough decision. When do you need to give them an answer?
W: By the end of the month. I'm going to discuss it with my family this weekend.
questions.explanation: The woman means she is indecisive about the transfer—she sees both advantages and disadvantages and hasn't made a decision yet.

options.text: She has already accepted the transfer.
She is undecided about the opportunity.

questions.stem: What does the man say about the photocopier?
questions.context:
W: I need to make a hundred copies of this handbook for the orientation.
M: The photocopier near the break room is out of toner. You'll need to use the one on the third floor.
W: Is there a lot of toner left in that one?
M: It was just refilled yesterday, so it should be fine.
questions.explanation: The man says the nearby photocopier is out of toner but the one on the third floor was recently refilled.

options.text: It has been replaced with a new model.
The nearest one is out of toner.
It is reserved for management use only.

questions.stem: What does the woman suggest about the marketing campaign?
questions.context:
M: I'm thinking of launching the new marketing campaign on social media first.
W: That could work, but I'd also suggest running a small test campaign before the full launch.
M: What kind of test do you have in mind?
W: A limited ad buy targeting a specific region. That way we can measure response before committing the full budget.
questions.explanation: The woman suggests running a small regional test campaign before committing to the full marketing launch.

options.text: Test it in a limited market first.

questions.stem: What does the woman want to know?
questions.context:
W: I noticed a charge on my corporate card that I don't recognize. It's from a company called TechServe.
M: That might be from the software subscription I renewed last month.
W: Could you confirm that? I need to make sure all charges are accounted for before I submit my expense report.
M: Let me check my records. I'll email you the invoice.
questions.explanation: The woman wants to confirm whether an unfamiliar charge on her corporate card is legitimate before submitting her expense report.

options.text: The source of an unfamiliar charge.
The name of a new vendor.

questions.stem: What does the man indicate about the warehouse?
questions.context:
W: How are things going at the new warehouse facility?
M: The setup is almost complete, but we're still waiting for the shelving units to be installed.
W: When do you expect them to be finished?
M: The contractor said by next Wednesday, but I wouldn't be surprised if it takes a few extra days.
questions.explanation: The man indicates that the warehouse setup is nearly complete but the shelving installation may take longer than the contractor estimated.

options.text: It is almost ready but behind schedule.
It is too small for current needs.

questions.stem: What does the woman need for the meeting?
questions.context:
W: I have a video conference with the overseas team at four o'clock. Can I use your webcam?
M: Sure, but the built-in microphone doesn't work very well. You might want to use a headset too.
W: Good to know. Do you have one I could borrow?
M: Yes, it's in my desk drawer. I'll bring it to you in a few minutes.
questions.explanation: The woman needs a webcam and headset for a video conference with the overseas team.

questions.stem: What does the man imply about the company policy?
questions.context:
W: Can I work from home next Friday? I have a plumber coming to fix a leak.
M: Normally that would be fine, but we just got a memo saying all employees need to be on-site for the next two weeks.
W: Is that because of the audit?
M: Yes, the auditors want to interview staff in person, so everyone needs to be available.
questions.explanation: The man implies that the normal work-from-home policy has been temporarily suspended due to an upcoming in-person audit.

options.text: It requires manager approval for overtime.

questions.stem: What does the woman say about the conference?
questions.context:
M: Are you planning to attend the technology conference in Seattle next month?
W: I'd like to, but the registration fee is quite expensive—six hundred dollars.
M: Really? I thought our department had a professional development budget for things like that.
W: It does, but we've already used most of it this quarter. I'll check if there's anything left.
questions.explanation: The woman says the conference registration fee is expensive and she's not sure if there's enough remaining budget to cover it.

options.text: She is presenting at the conference.
The company is sponsoring the event.

questions.stem: Why does the woman mention the focus group?
questions.context:
M: Our website traffic has dropped significantly over the last three months.
W: We ran a focus group last week, and participants said the site is hard to navigate.
M: Did they have specific suggestions for improvement?
W: Several people mentioned the search function and the menu layout. I'll share the full report with you.
questions.explanation: The woman mentions the focus group to provide evidence that user experience issues are likely causing the drop in website traffic.

options.text: To explain why traffic has decreased.
To announce a new product feature.

questions.stem: What did the woman do yesterday?
questions.context:
M: How did the factory tour with the investors go yesterday?
W: It went really well. They were impressed by our automated assembly line.
M: Did they ask about expanding production capacity?
W: Yes, and I showed them the plans for the new wing we're building.
questions.explanation: Yesterday, the woman gave investors a tour of the factory and showed them expansion plans.

options.text: She gave investors a factory tour.

questions.stem: What does the man think about the proposal?
questions.context:
W: I've drafted a proposal to offer flexible working hours for all employees.
M: I think it's a great idea in principle, but we need to address how it would work for departments that require shift coverage.
W: That's a fair point. Maybe we could offer it only to departments where coverage isn't an issue.
M: That could work. Let's discuss it with the department heads first.
questions.explanation: The man thinks the proposal is good overall but has practical concerns about departments that need continuous shift coverage.

options.text: It is good but needs some adjustments.
It is not relevant to their company.

questions.stem: What does the man plan to do this afternoon?
questions.context:
W: The client samples arrived this morning. Have you had a chance to inspect them?
M: Not yet, but I'm planning to go through them after lunch with the quality team.
W: Let me know if any of them don't meet our specifications.
M: Will do. I'll have a report ready by five o'clock.
questions.explanation: The man plans to inspect the client samples with the quality team this afternoon and prepare a report by five o'clock.

questions.stem: What does the woman say about the branch office?
questions.context:
M: How's the expansion going at the branch office in Portland?
W: It's been slower than expected. We've had trouble finding qualified candidates for the technical positions.
M: Have you tried working with a recruiting agency?
W: We started working with one last week. They've already sent over a few promising resumes.
questions.explanation: The woman says the branch office expansion has been delayed due to difficulty hiring qualified technical staff, though a recruiting agency is now helping.

options.text: It has exceeded its sales targets.
It recently moved to a new location.

questions.stem: What are the speakers planning?
questions.context:
M: We should organize a team lunch for Jennifer's promotion. She's been with the company for ten years.
W: That's a wonderful idea. Should we do it at a restaurant or order in?
M: A restaurant would be nicer. How about that Italian place she likes?
W: Perfect. I'll make a reservation for Friday. What time works for the team?
questions.explanation: The speakers are planning a team lunch at a restaurant to celebrate Jennifer's promotion.

options.text: A celebration lunch for a colleague.
A farewell party for a departing employee.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the speakers?
questions.context:
W: I noticed the new security system requires fingerprint scanning to enter the building.
M: Yes, all employees need to register their fingerprints at the security desk by Friday.
W: Is this replacing the key card system entirely?
M: Not completely. Key cards will still work for the parking garage and gym, but building access will require fingerprint verification.
questions.explanation: The speakers are employees at the same company and are discussing changes to the building's security access system.

options.text: They work at a security company.
They are colleagues at the same office.
They are installing the security system.
They are visiting the building for the first time.

questions.stem: What does the woman tell the man?
questions.context:
M: Could you tell me when the next express bus to downtown leaves?
W: The next one departs at two fifteen from Bay Three.
M: And how long does it take to reach the central station?
W: About twenty-five minutes, unless there's heavy traffic.
questions.explanation: The woman tells the man that the next express bus leaves at 2:15 from Bay Three and the ride takes about 25 minutes.

options.text: The bus service has been suspended.
The departure time and travel duration.

questions.stem: What does the man recommend about the product?
questions.context:
W: Which laptop model would you recommend for someone who mostly does graphic design?
M: For graphic design, I'd suggest the ProLine X1. It has a high-resolution display and a powerful graphics card.
W: How does it compare in price to the CompactPro series?
M: It's about two hundred dollars more, but the display quality is significantly better for design work.
questions.explanation: The man recommends the ProLine X1 because its high-resolution display and graphics card are better suited for graphic design.

options.text: A model with superior display quality.

questions.stem: What will the man do before the end of the day?
questions.context:
W: Have you finished writing the job descriptions for the new positions?
M: I've completed two out of four. I should have the others done before I leave today.
W: Great, because I want to post them on the job boards tomorrow morning.
M: I'll email you the final versions by five thirty at the latest.
questions.explanation: The man will finish writing the remaining job descriptions and email them by 5:30 PM.

questions.stem: What does the woman say about the survey results?
questions.context:
M: Did the employee satisfaction survey reveal anything unexpected?
W: Actually, yes. While overall satisfaction is high, a large number of respondents said they want more opportunities for professional growth.
M: That's interesting. Any ideas on how to address that?
W: I think we could expand our internal training programs and create a mentorship initiative.
questions.explanation: The woman says the survey showed high overall satisfaction but also revealed a strong demand for more professional development opportunities.

options.text: The response rate was very low.

questions.stem: Where does this conversation take place?
questions.context:
M: I'd like to deposit this check into my savings account, please.
W: Of course. Do you have your account number?
M: Yes, it's on my debit card. Here you go.
W: Thank you. The deposit will be available by tomorrow morning.
questions.explanation: The conversation takes place at a bank, as the man is making a deposit into his savings account with the help of a teller.

questions.stem: Why does the woman say, 'Let's not get ahead of ourselves'?
questions.context:
M: This new product could be a huge success. Maybe we should start planning international distribution right away.
W: Let's not get ahead of ourselves. We haven't even launched domestically yet.
M: You're right. I just got excited by the early test results.
W: I understand, but let's focus on a strong domestic launch first, and then consider expanding.
questions.explanation: The woman says this to caution against planning too far ahead—they should focus on the domestic launch before thinking about international distribution.

options.text: She wants to cancel the product launch.
She thinks they should proceed step by step.
She disagrees with the test results.
She wants to hire more staff first.

questions.stem: What does the man say he received?
questions.context:
M: I just got a call from the landlord. He's increasing the office rent by ten percent starting in April.
W: That's a significant increase. Can we negotiate?
M: I tried, but he said the rates are non-negotiable.
W: We might need to consider looking at other office spaces in the area.
questions.explanation: The man says he received a call from the landlord about a ten-percent rent increase.

options.text: A notice of a rent increase.

questions.stem: What does the woman want to improve?
questions.context:
W: I've been thinking about how we can make our onboarding process more effective.
M: What changes do you have in mind?
W: Currently, new hires spend the first week just reading manuals. I want to add hands-on workshops and assign each person a mentor from their team.
M: That would definitely make the experience more engaging. Want me to help design the workshop curriculum?
questions.explanation: The woman wants to improve the employee onboarding process by adding hands-on workshops and a mentorship component.

questions.stem: What did the man forget to do?
questions.context:
W: Did you send the invitations for the board meeting next week?
M: Oh, I completely forgot. I've been so busy with the quarterly review.
W: You should send them today—the board members need at least five days' notice.
M: I'll do it right now and include the agenda as an attachment.
questions.explanation: The man forgot to send the invitations for the board meeting. He plans to send them immediately.

questions.stem: What does the man say about the merger?
questions.context:
W: Have you heard any updates about the merger with Hargrove Industries?
M: The deal is still in negotiations. Both sides are trying to agree on the valuation.
W: Do you think it will go through?
M: It's hard to say. If they can't agree on terms by the end of the quarter, the whole thing could fall apart.
questions.explanation: The man says the merger is still being negotiated and could fail if the two sides cannot agree on valuation terms in time.

options.text: It is uncertain and still in negotiation.

questions.stem: What does the woman ask about the hotel?
questions.context:
W: I need to book a hotel for the conference in Miami. Does the company have a preferred hotel?
M: Yes, we have a corporate rate at the Bayfront Suites. It's very close to the convention center.
W: How much is the corporate rate?
M: It's one hundred and forty-five dollars per night, which includes breakfast.
questions.explanation: The woman asks whether the company has a preferred hotel for her trip and inquires about the corporate rate.

options.text: The company's preferred hotel and rate.

questions.stem: What has the woman been working on?
questions.context:
M: You look exhausted. Is everything okay?
W: I've been working on the environmental impact assessment for the new factory site. It's a massive report.
M: Do you need any help with the research portion?
W: That would be amazing. I still need data on local water quality and wildlife habitats.
questions.explanation: The woman has been working on an environmental impact assessment for a proposed new factory location.

options.text: A budget forecast for next quarter.

questions.stem: What does the woman say about the order?
questions.context:
M: I notice we're running low on printer paper. Should I place an order?
W: I actually placed one yesterday. It should arrive by Thursday.
M: Great. Did you also order ink cartridges? We're almost out of those too.
W: Yes, I ordered both. The invoice is on your desk if you want to check it.
questions.explanation: The woman says she already placed an order for printer paper and ink cartridges yesterday, and it should arrive by Thursday.

options.text: It was delivered with the wrong items.
It was cancelled due to budget cuts.

questions.stem: What does the man mean when he says, 'That's a tall order'?
questions.context:
W: The CEO wants us to increase customer retention by twenty-five percent in the next six months.
M: That's a tall order. We'd need to completely overhaul our loyalty program.
W: I agree, but I think it's achievable if we focus on personalization and faster response times.
M: You're right. Let's start by analyzing the current churn data.
questions.explanation: When the man says 'That's a tall order,' he means the goal is very ambitious and challenging to achieve in the given timeframe.

options.text: The CEO has a new directive.

questions.stem: What is the woman's problem?
questions.context:
W: I've been waiting for a call back from the insurance company for three days.
M: That's frustrating. Have you tried reaching them through their online chat?
W: I didn't know they had one. Where can I find it?
M: Go to their website and look for the 'Contact Us' section. The chat option is usually available during business hours.
questions.explanation: The woman's problem is that the insurance company hasn't returned her calls for three days.

questions.stem: What does the woman say about the workshop location?
questions.context:
M: I was thinking of holding the customer service workshop at the downtown training center.
W: Actually, I'd suggest doing it at the Lakeside Conference Center instead. It has better audio-visual equipment and the rooms are more spacious.
M: Good point, but isn't Lakeside more expensive?
W: A little, but the quality of the facilities makes it worth the extra cost.
questions.explanation: The woman recommends the Lakeside Conference Center because it has better AV equipment and more spacious rooms, despite being slightly more expensive.

options.text: It is too far from the office.
It is fully booked for the month.

questions.stem: What does the man say about the delivery?
questions.context:
W: Were you expecting a delivery from Pacific Office Supplies today?
M: Yes, I ordered new desk organizers for the team. They were supposed to arrive by noon.
W: The receptionist said a package arrived about an hour ago. It's at the front desk.
M: Great, I'll go pick it up. Thanks for letting me know.
questions.explanation: The man says he ordered desk organizers that were expected to arrive today, and they've arrived at the front desk.

options.text: It was sent to the wrong address.
It has arrived at the front desk.
It was delayed by a week.

questions.stem: What does the woman suggest for future events?
questions.context:
M: The turnout at last week's networking event was lower than expected.
W: I think we need to promote these events earlier. We only sent out invitations a week before.
M: You're right. What lead time do you think we need?
W: At least three weeks. That gives people enough time to clear their schedules.
questions.explanation: The woman suggests promoting events with at least three weeks' advance notice to improve attendance.

options.text: Reduce the number of events per year.

questions.stem: What does the woman ask about the form?
questions.context:
W: I'm filling out the new vendor registration form. Do I need to include a tax identification number?
M: Yes, that's required for all vendors. It's in section three of the form.
W: Do we also need to attach a copy of our business license?
M: Yes, along with proof of insurance. Everything is listed in the checklist at the end.
questions.explanation: The woman asks whether a tax identification number and business license are needed for the vendor registration form.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the company?
questions.context:
M: I see we've opened three new locations in the past year alone.
W: Yes, and there are plans for two more in the first quarter of next year.
M: The demand has really exceeded our forecasts. Are we hiring enough staff to keep up?
W: HR is running accelerated recruiting campaigns in all new markets.
questions.explanation: The company has been expanding rapidly, opening multiple new locations and actively recruiting to meet growing demand.

options.text: It is merging with a competitor.
It is moving to a new headquarters.

questions.stem: What does the man want to confirm?
questions.context:
M: The annual health screening is scheduled for next Thursday, right?
W: Yes, from nine A.M. to three P.M. in the first-floor conference room.
M: Do I need to make an appointment, or is it walk-in?
W: It's walk-in, but mornings tend to be less crowded if you want to avoid waiting.
questions.explanation: The man wants to confirm the date and logistics of the annual health screening.

options.text: The details of a health screening.

questions.stem: What does the woman imply about the project timeline?
questions.context:
M: The client approved the project timeline, so we're all set to start development next week.
W: That's great, but I noticed the testing phase is only scheduled for one week. That seems too short for a project this complex.
M: You think we need more time for testing?
W: Definitely. If we find bugs late in the process, fixing them under time pressure could lead to more problems.
questions.explanation: The woman implies that one week of testing is insufficient for the project's complexity and could risk quality issues.

options.text: The testing phase should be longer.
It has been approved by all stakeholders.

questions.stem: What is the man having trouble with?
questions.context:
M: I can't seem to connect to the wireless network in the meeting room.
W: Have you tried the guest network? The password is on the whiteboard.
M: I tried that, but it keeps saying 'authentication failed.'
W: Let me call IT. There might be an issue with the access point in that room.
questions.explanation: The man is having trouble connecting to the wireless network in the meeting room.

questions.stem: What does the man think about the training budget?
questions.context:
W: We've allocated fifteen thousand dollars for professional training this year.
M: Given the size of our department, that might not cover everyone. We have twenty-five employees.
W: Do you think we should request a larger budget?
M: Either that, or we prioritize based on development goals and rotate who attends each quarter.
questions.explanation: The man thinks the training budget may be insufficient for the department size and suggests either increasing it or prioritizing participants.

options.text: It may not be sufficient for everyone.
It should be used for equipment instead.
It was reduced from last year.

questions.stem: What does the woman tell the man about the event?
questions.context:
M: Are there any seats left for the guest speaker event this Friday?
W: I'm sorry, but the event is fully booked. We had to close registration yesterday.
M: That's disappointing. Will the presentation be recorded?
W: Yes, a recording will be posted on the company intranet by Monday.
questions.explanation: The woman tells the man the event is fully booked but a recording will be available on the intranet.

options.text: It has been moved to a larger room.

questions.stem: What does the man suggest about the budget report?
questions.context:
W: I finished the draft of the annual budget report, but the projections section still needs work.
M: Why don't you ask someone from the analytics team to review the numbers? They're really good with forecasting models.
W: That's a great idea. Do you know who's available this week?
M: Alex mentioned he has some free time. I'll introduce you.
questions.explanation: The man suggests having someone from the analytics team review the projections section of the budget report.

options.text: Get analytics support for the forecasting.
Use last year's numbers as a placeholder.

questions.stem: What does the woman need to do before the trip?
questions.context:
M: Are you all set for the business trip to Berlin next week?
W: Almost. I still need to get my travel visa processed. I submitted the application but haven't heard back yet.
M: You might want to check the processing time on the consulate's website.
W: I'll do that today. I also need to book a hotel once the visa is confirmed.
questions.explanation: The woman needs to get her travel visa processed and book a hotel before the business trip to Berlin.

options.text: Obtain a travel visa and book a hotel.

questions.stem: What does the man want to change about the newsletter?
questions.context:
M: I think our monthly newsletter could use a refresh. The current design looks a bit dated.
W: I agree. What changes are you thinking of?
M: A cleaner layout, more visuals, and an interactive section where readers can submit questions or feedback.
W: That sounds like a big improvement. Should I get a quote from the design agency?
questions.explanation: The man wants to update the newsletter with a cleaner layout, more visuals, and an interactive reader section.

options.text: Reduce it from monthly to quarterly.
Modernize its design and add interactivity.

questions.stem: What did the man do last week?
questions.context:
W: How did the visit to the new supplier's factory in Osaka go?
M: It was very productive. Their production quality is excellent, and they can meet our volume requirements.
W: Did you discuss pricing?
M: Yes, they offered us a competitive rate. I'll present the details at our team meeting tomorrow.
questions.explanation: Last week, the man visited a new supplier's factory in Osaka to evaluate production quality and discuss pricing.

questions.stem: What does the woman plan to propose?
questions.context:
W: I've been reviewing our customer feedback, and I noticed a recurring theme—people want faster delivery options.
M: That's not surprising. Many of our competitors now offer next-day delivery.
W: I'm going to propose that we partner with a third-party logistics provider to offer expedited shipping.
M: Good idea. Do you have any companies in mind?
W: I've been researching a few. I'll have a recommendation ready by next week.
questions.explanation: The woman plans to propose partnering with a third-party logistics provider to offer faster delivery to customers.
